description: Divergence-corpus vectors added by acdp/0.2.0 (RFC-ACDP-0001 Appendix A). Pins the hash-divergence sources not already covered by can-001..011: (a) acdp_version omitted vs explicit '0.1.0' vs explicit '0.2.0' — three distinct preimages, all valid on the wire; 0.2.0 producers MUST emit the field explicitly (RFC-ACDP-0001 §6); (b) microsecond-precision timestamps (can-006 covers nanoseconds) — producers MUST truncate to milliseconds or reject; (c) null-vs-absent-vs-empty: a null-valued metadata member, an empty metadata object, and an absent metadata field are three distinct preimages. Together with can-001..011 these form the divergence corpus every SDK must pass (see the fixture-ID table in RFC-ACDP-0001 Appendix A). Executed arithmetically by scripts/conformance-runner.py.

vectors.name: acdp_version omitted — valid 0.1.0 wire form (baseline preimage)
vectors.rationale: Interpretation rule: absence is read as '0.1.0' but the absent field is never materialized — a verifier that injects a default acdp_version before hashing produces the explicit-form hash below and raises a false hash_mismatch (RFC-ACDP-0001 §6).

vectors.name: acdp_version '0.1.0' explicit — semantically identical to omitted, byte-distinct preimage
vectors.rationale: Conversely, a verifier that strips acdp_version before hashing produces the omitted-form hash above. The hash binds the bytes: a producer picks one form and signs exactly what it emits.

vectors.name: acdp_version '0.2.0' explicit — the form 0.2.0 producers MUST emit
vectors.rationale: 0.2.0 builder conformance check: a builder targeting 0.2.0 MUST default to emitting the field; the omission default of RFC-ACDP-0001 §6 is closed for 0.2.0 builders.

vectors.name: Microsecond-precision expires_at — DIVERGENT (producers MUST truncate to milliseconds)
vectors.rationale: Python datetime defaults land exactly here (microseconds), where Go/Rust/Java clocks land on can-006's nanosecond case. A conformant producer pipeline either pre-truncates (producing the millisecond hash below) or rejects the input.

vectors.name: Millisecond-truncated expires_at — CANONICAL (the expected post-truncation hash)
metadata: {} — empty object is a distinct preimage from absent metadata
vectors.rationale: An SDK builder that materializes an empty metadata object 'for convenience' changes the hash relative to vector 1 (absent metadata). Same rule as can-005's tags case, pinned here for objects.

vectors.name: metadata: {"note": null} — a null-valued member is a third distinct preimage
vectors.rationale: JCS preserves null members inside producer data; serializers that strip null-valued members (e.g. 'omitempty'-style defaults) silently change the hash. Note this concerns null INSIDE producer-controlled data — top-level optional wire fields still follow the absent-vs-null convention (omitted, never null; RFC-ACDP-0005 §2.2.1, schema-011..014).
